attributes.name: content
value:
Lab API User Guide
We have re-worked the Pega Labs scripts to allow some of the Self-Service portal operations to be performed via API calls. We also provide scripts in /home/teuser/scripts that call the API and can be used as examples.

Before you begin:

All scripts are located in the /home/teuser/scripts directory.

All script must be executed as teuser

Scripts will make API calls and wait, and some operations may take a while, so you might want to use tmux to start a session you can reattach to in case you lose connection.

You won't see progress while running the script, but you can watch logs in /var/log/pegalabs/portal in a separate session if you want to.

When the script finishes, a success or failure email will be sent to the emails associated with the system.

Please refer to the SSH Guide for SSH connection information.

API scripts
update.sh
Update the Pega Platform to a specific version and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the update.

Parameters
--PlatformVersion
Desired Pega platform version. Must adhere to the Pega Labs naming convention:

major.minor.patch-suffix

where suffix is one of: GA, B[n], HEAD[n], DEV[n], or latest.

For example: 8.7.0-GA, 8.24.0-DEV1234, 8.23.2-HEAD1234, 8.24.0-latest.

Examples
./update.sh --PlatformVersion 8.8.4-GA
./update.sh --PlatformVersion 8.24.0-latest
./update.sh --PlatformVersion latest
install.sh
Reinstalls the Pega Platform with a specific version and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the install.

Parameters
--PlatformVersion same as with update.

--DisableSecureInstall [true|<no input>]

Disables secure platform install feature (Secure Platform feature is enabled by default in versions 7.4.0 and higher.

--ProductionLevel [1-5|no input]
Sets the Production level of the system during an update or install. Default is 2 (development)

Example
./install.sh --PlatformVersion 8.23.1-GA --DisableSecureInstall true --ProductionLevel 5
clear-cache.sh
Clears the platform cache, restart the application server and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the restart.

Parameters
--clear-database-cache
Clears the database cache tables as well as the system cache.

--clear-cassandra-data
Clears Cassandra as well (used by CDH).

Example
./clear-cache.sh --clear-database-cache --clear-cassandra-data
Restart
This operation will restart the application server and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the restart.

Example
./restart.sh
database-snapshot.sh
Take a local snapshot of the database, Pega webapps, and Pega app contexts) and save it in the /var/lib/pega/backup directory.

Parameters
--snapshot-description <text> - Text description to identify the snapshot.
Example
./database-snapshot.sh --snapshot-description "Test123"
framework.sh
Perform an update or install of a GA version of any Pega Strategic applications offered in Pega Labs. Please see your Self-Service portal for a list of the available strategic apps.

Parameters
--Action [install/update]
Desired action to perform. install or update a strategic App

--AppName [application name]
Application Name in Pega Labs required format (CRM, PegaMKT, PegaFFS, etc). Check the Strategic Apps dropdown list on your Self-Service Portal for a current list of available Applications

--AppVersion [#.##]
Application Version in Pega Labs required format (7.22, 7.31, 7.41, etc). Check the Strategic Apps dropdown list on your Self-Service Portal for a current list of available versions.

--ImportSampleData [true|no input]
Imports the framework sample data (if present).

--DisableSecureInstall [true|no input]
Disables secure platform install feature - Setting to true will prevent operators from being disabled when a RAP is imported.

Example
./framework.sh --Action install --AppName PegaFFS --AppVersion 7.32 --ImportSampleData true
framework-dev.sh
Performs an update or install of a pre-release or daily version of any Pega Strategic applications supported in Pega Labs. If there is tailoring or custom code required for your specific app, the script should be copied and re-named relative to the strategic app so it does not get overwritten when Pega Labs pushes changes.

Parameters
Same as framework.sh plus:--BuildRepoURL [URL]
Full URL Path for the Strategic App Build Zip file

--BuildRepoUser [username]
Username for the Strategic App Build Repo (If password-protected).

--BuildRepoPassword [password]
Password for the Strategic App Build Repo (If password-protected).

Example
./framework-dev.sh --Action update --AppName PegaMarketing --AppVersion 8.10 --BuildRepoURL http://vnbamartifactory01x:8081/artifactory/CRM/8.1/builds/PegaMarketing/latest/PegaMarketing_0801.zip
udf.sh
Add or remove UDFs (user-defined functions) to the database.

Parameters
--UdfAction [Install|Remove]
Desired UDF action Install or Remove UDFs (User Defined Functions).

Example
./udf.sh --UdfAction Remove

chunks.content:
DOC: Lab API. Lab API User Guide
We have re-worked the Pega Labs scripts to allow some of the Self-Service portal operations to be performed via API calls. We also provide scripts in /home/teuser/scripts that call the API and can be used as examples.

Before you begin:

All scripts are located in the /home/teuser/scripts directory.

All script must be executed as teuser

Scripts will make API calls and wait, and some operations may take a while, so you might want to use tmux to start a session you can reattach to in case you lose connection.

You won't see progress while running the script, but you can watch logs in /var/log/pegalabs/portal in a separate session if you want to.

When the script finishes, a success or failure email will be sent to the emails associated with the system.

Please refer to the SSH Guide for SSH connection information.

Lab API User Guide
We have re-worked the Pega Labs scripts to allow some of the Self-Service portal operations to be performed via API calls. We also provide scripts in /home/teuser/scripts that call the API and can be used as examples.

Before you begin:

All scripts are located in the /home/teuser/scripts directory.

All script must be executed as teuser

Scripts will make API calls and wait, and some operations may take a while, so you might want to use tmux to start a session you can reattach to in case you lose connection.

You won't see progress while running the script, but you can watch logs in /var/log/pegalabs/portal in a separate session if you want to.

When the script finishes, a success or failure email will be sent to the emails associated with the system.

Please refer to the SSH Guide for SSH connection information.

API scripts
update.sh
Update the Pega Platform to a specific version and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the update.

Parameters
--PlatformVersion
Desired Pega platform version. Must adhere to the Pega Labs naming convention:

major.minor.patch-suffix

where suffix is one of: GA, B[n], HEAD[n], DEV[n], or latest.

For example: 8.7.0-GA, 8.24.0-DEV1234, 8.23.2-HEAD1234, 8.24.0-latest.

Examples
./update.sh --PlatformVersion 8.8.4-GA
./update.sh --PlatformVersion 8.24.0-latest
./update.sh --PlatformVersion latest
install.sh
Reinstalls the Pega Platform with a specific version and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the install.

Parameters
--PlatformVersion same as with update.

--DisableSecureInstall [true|<no input>]

Disables secure platform install feature (Secure Platform feature is enabled by default in versions 7.4.0 and higher.

--ProductionLevel [1-5|no input]
Sets the Production level of the system during an update or install. Default is 2 (development)

Example
./install.sh --PlatformVersion 8.23.1-GA --DisableSecureInstall true --ProductionLevel 5
clear-cache.sh
Clears the platform cache, restart the application server and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the restart.

Parameters
--clear-database-cache
Clears the database cache tables as well as the system cache.

--clear-cassandra-data
Clears Cassandra as well (used by CDH).

Example
./clear-cache.sh --clear-database-cache --clear-cassandra-data
Restart
This operation will restart the application server and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the restart.

Example
./restart.sh
database-snapshot.sh
Take a local snapshot of the database, Pega webapps, and Pega app contexts) and save it in the /var/lib/pega/backup directory.

Parameters
--snapshot-description <text> - Text description to identify the snapshot.
Example
./database-snapshot.sh --snapshot-description "Test123"
framework.sh
Perform an update or install of a GA version of any Pega Strategic applications offered in Pega Labs. Please see your Self-Service portal for a list of the available strategic apps.

Parameters
--Action [install/update]
Desired action to perform. install or update a strategic App

--AppName [application name]
Application Name in Pega Labs required format (CRM, PegaMKT, PegaFFS, etc). Check the Strategic Apps dropdown list on your Self-Service Portal for a current list of available Applications

--AppVersion [#.##]
Application Version in Pega Labs required format (7.22, 7.31, 7.41, etc). Check the Strategic Apps dropdown list on your Self-Service Portal for a current list of available versions.

--ImportSampleData [true|no input]
Imports the framework sample data (if present).

--DisableSecureInstall [true|no input]
Disables secure platform install feature - Setting to true will prevent operators from being disabled when a RAP is imported.

Example
./framework.sh --Action install --AppName PegaFFS --AppVersion 7.32 --ImportSampleData true
framework-dev.sh
Performs an update or install of a pre-release or daily version of any Pega Strategic applications supported in Pega Labs. If there is tailoring or custom code required for your specific app, the script should be copied and re-named relative to the strategic app so it does not get overwritten when Pega Labs pushes changes.

Parameters
Same as framework.sh plus:--BuildRepoURL [URL]
Full URL Path for the Strategic App Build Zip file

--BuildRepoUser [username]
Username for the Strategic App Build Repo (If password-protected).

--BuildRepoPassword [password]
Password for the Strategic App Build Repo (If password-protected).

Example
./framework-dev.sh --Action update --AppName PegaMarketing --AppVersion 8.10 --BuildRepoURL http://vnbamartifactory01x:8081/artifactory/CRM/8.1/builds/PegaMarketing/latest/PegaMarketing_0801.zip
udf.sh
Add or remove UDFs (user-defined functions) to the database.

Parameters
--UdfAction [Install|Remove]
Desired UDF action Install or Remove UDFs (User Defined Functions).

Example
./udf.sh --UdfAction Remove

value:
Lab API User Guide
We have re-worked the Pega Labs scripts to allow some of the Self-Service portal operations to be performed via API calls. We also provide scripts in /home/teuser/scripts that call the API and can be used as examples.

Before you begin:

All scripts are located in the /home/teuser/scripts directory.

All script must be executed as teuser

Scripts will make API calls and wait, and some operations may take a while, so you might want to use tmux to start a session you can reattach to in case you lose connection.

You won't see progress while running the script, but you can watch logs in /var/log/pegalabs/portal in a separate session if you want to.

When the script finishes, a success or failure email will be sent to the emails associated with the system.

Please refer to the SSH Guide for SSH connection information.

API scripts
update.sh
Update the Pega Platform to a specific version and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the update.

Parameters
--PlatformVersion
Desired Pega platform version. Must adhere to the Pega Labs naming convention:

major.minor.patch-suffix

where suffix is one of: GA, B[n], HEAD[n], DEV[n], or latest.

For example: 8.7.0-GA, 8.24.0-DEV1234, 8.23.2-HEAD1234, 8.24.0-latest.

Examples
./update.sh --PlatformVersion 8.8.4-GA
./update.sh --PlatformVersion 8.24.0-latest
./update.sh --PlatformVersion latest
install.sh
Reinstalls the Pega Platform with a specific version and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the install.

Parameters
--PlatformVersion same as with update.

--DisableSecureInstall [true|<no input>]

Disables secure platform install feature (Secure Platform feature is enabled by default in versions 7.4.0 and higher.

--ProductionLevel [1-5|no input]
Sets the Production level of the system during an update or install. Default is 2 (development)

Example
./install.sh --PlatformVersion 8.23.1-GA --DisableSecureInstall true --ProductionLevel 5
clear-cache.sh
Clears the platform cache, restart the application server and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the restart.

Parameters
--clear-database-cache
Clears the database cache tables as well as the system cache.

--clear-cassandra-data
Clears Cassandra as well (used by CDH).

Example
./clear-cache.sh --clear-database-cache --clear-cassandra-data
Restart
This operation will restart the application server and then check to make sure the Designer Studio is up and accessible after the restart.

Example
./restart.sh
database-snapshot.sh
Take a local snapshot of the database, Pega webapps, and Pega app contexts) and save it in the /var/lib/pega/backup directory.

Parameters
--snapshot-description <text> - Text description to identify the snapshot.
Example
./database-snapshot.sh --snapshot-description "Test123"
framework.sh
Perform an update or install of a GA version of any Pega Strategic applications offered in Pega Labs. Please see your Self-Service portal for a list of the available strategic apps.

Parameters
--Action [install/update]
Desired action to perform. install or update a strategic App

--AppName [application name]
Application Name in Pega Labs required format (CRM, PegaMKT, PegaFFS, etc). Check the Strategic Apps dropdown list on your Self-Service Portal for a current list of available Applications

--AppVersion [#.##]
Application Version in Pega Labs required format (7.22, 7.31, 7.41, etc). Check the Strategic Apps dropdown list on your Self-Service Portal for a current list of available versions.

--ImportSampleData [true|no input]
Imports the framework sample data (if present).

--DisableSecureInstall [true|no input]
Disables secure platform install feature - Setting to true will prevent operators from being disabled when a RAP is imported.

Example
./framework.sh --Action install --AppName PegaFFS --AppVersion 7.32 --ImportSampleData true
framework-dev.sh
Performs an update or install of a pre-release or daily version of any Pega Strategic applications supported in Pega Labs. If there is tailoring or custom code required for your specific app, the script should be copied and re-named relative to the strategic app so it does not get overwritten when Pega Labs pushes changes.

Parameters
Same as framework.sh plus:--BuildRepoURL [URL]
Full URL Path for the Strategic App Build Zip file

--BuildRepoUser [username]
Username for the Strategic App Build Repo (If password-protected).

--BuildRepoPassword [password]
Password for the Strategic App Build Repo (If password-protected).

Example
./framework-dev.sh --Action update --AppName PegaMarketing --AppVersion 8.10 --BuildRepoURL http://vnbamartifactory01x:8081/artifactory/CRM/8.1/builds/PegaMarketing/latest/PegaMarketing_0801.zip
udf.sh
Add or remove UDFs (user-defined functions) to the database.

Parameters
--UdfAction [Install|Remove]
Desired UDF action Install or Remove UDFs (User Defined Functions).

Example
./udf.sh --UdfAction Remove
